skill.name: copilot-coding-agent
skill.description: GitHub Copilot Coding Agent automation. Apply the ai-copilot label to an issue → GitHub Actions auto-assigns Copilot via GraphQL → Copilot creates a Draft PR. One-click issue-to-PR pipeline.
skill.summary: Automate GitHub Copilot Coding Agent to create Draft PRs from issues with a single label
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 48 static findings are false positives. The external_commands detections are backticks in markdown code fences (documentation examples, not executable code). The network detections are legitimate GitHub documentation URLs. The cryptographic algorithm warnings are misclassifications. This is a legitimate documentation skill for GitHub Copilot automation with no malicious code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected backticks in markdown code fences as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are documentation examples in code blocks, not executable code. The skill describes bash commands but does not execute them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All backticks are within markdown code fences (```bash ... ```) used for documentation. No actual code execution occurs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URL Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected URLs as 'network' activity. These are legitimate GitHub documentation reference links in the References section, not network calls made by the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: URLs are static documentation links to official GitHub docs. No network requests are made by this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged content in SKILL.toon as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is a misclassification - the file contains only requirement text about Copilot plans and permissions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.toon contains plain text requirements documentation. No cryptographic code or algorithms present.

content.user_title: Automate Copilot to Create PRs from Issues
content.value_statement: This skill automates GitHub Copilot Coding Agent to transform issues into Draft Pull Requests with a single label. Team members add the ai-copilot label, and Copilot handles implementation automatically.
actual_capabilities: Automatically assign GitHub issues to Copilot Coding Agent via label
Trigger GitHub Actions workflow to set Copilot as issue assignee
Create Draft Pull Requests with Copilot-generated code
Integrate with external systems like Jira via webhooks
limitations: Requires GitHub Copilot Pro+, Business, or Enterprise plan
First PR from Copilot requires manual approval by a team member with write access
Personal Access Token with repo scope must be configured as a secret
Copilot output quality depends on issue description detail

use_cases.title: Automate backlog processing
use_cases.description: PMs create issues with the ai-copilot label, and Copilot automatically implements refactors, documentation, or tests without developer involvement.

use_cases.title: Delegation workflow
use_cases.description: Offload routine coding tasks to Copilot so developers focus on complex features. Issues labeled with ai-copilot route to Copilot automatically.

use_cases.title: CI/CD pipeline integration
use_cases.description: Connect external systems like Jira or Zapier to automatically create GitHub issues that Copilot then implements, creating a fully automated pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Setup Copilot automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the one-time setup to configure Copilot Coding Agent automation. Execute: bash scripts/copilot-setup-workflow.sh

prompt_templates.title: Create new Copilot task
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new GitHub issue with the ai-copilot label to trigger Copilot implementation. Use: gh issue create --label ai-copilot --title "Your task title" --body "Detailed description"

prompt_templates.title: Assign existing issue to Copilot
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the ai-copilot label to an existing issue to trigger Copilot. Use: gh issue edit ISSUE_NUMBER --add-label ai-copilot

prompt_templates.title: Check Copilot results
prompt_templates.prompt: List all Pull Requests created by Copilot and check their status. Use: gh pr list --search 'head:copilot/'

output_examples.input: gh issue create --label ai-copilot --title "Add user authentication" --body "Implement JWT-based auth with refresh tokens"
output_examples.output: Issue #42 created. GitHub Actions automatically assigns Copilot. Within minutes, Copilot creates a branch, implements the auth code, and opens a Draft PR for review.
Setup complete. COPILOT_ASSIGN_TOKEN secret registered, assign-to-copilot.yml workflow deployed, ai-copilot label created.

best_practices: Write detailed issue descriptions to improve Copilot code output quality
Review Draft PRs promptly since Copilot is treated as an external contributor
Use the ai-copilot label consistently to build a reliable automation workflow
anti_patterns: Adding the label without a clear issue description will produce poor results
Skipping manual approval on the first PR can block the workflow
Using PATs with excessive permissions creates unnecessary security risk

faq.question: What GitHub plan do I need for this skill?
faq.answer: You need GitHub Copilot Pro+, Business, or Enterprise. The Copilot Coding Agent feature is not available on free or legacy plans.

faq.question: Why does the first Copilot PR need manual approval?
faq.answer: Copilot is treated like an external contributor. Someone with write access must approve the first PR before CI can run automatically.

faq.question: Can Copilot access my private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, when enabled in repository settings, Copilot Coding Agent can read and write to your private repositories within the assigned scope.

faq.question: What permissions does the PAT need?
faq.answer: The Personal Access Token requires repo scope to create branches, commit code, and open pull requests on behalf of the automation.

faq.question: How long does Copilot take to create a PR?
faq.answer: Typically 5-15 minutes depending on task complexity. The workflow triggers immediately, but Copilot needs time to analyze the issue and write code.

faq.question: Can I use this with Jira or other project tools?
faq.answer: Yes, configure Jira or Zapier webhooks to create GitHub issues with the ai-copilot label. This creates a fully automated pipeline from issue creation to PR.
